Both external tools returned no usable amyloid-kinetics results (ML snippet search gave only unrelated ML papers; web_search returned zero hits) — consistent with prior runs noting this environment lacks access to the domain literature. I'll ground the structure in the established Cohen/Meisl/Knowles master-equation framework plus the Langmuir binding isotherm from first principles.

A key mechanistic insight from that framework worth reflecting: inhibitors that act by **binding fibril ends/surfaces** target microscopic rate constants (k₊, k₂) *differently* from those that sequester free monomer. The most robust, parsimonious encoding that captures the dominant coupled amplitude+timing signature is monomer sequestration propagated through mass balance — exactly the breakthrough.
